title: Obama Presidential Library: Records Regarding the Death of Eduard Shevardnadze (July 2014)

communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Freedom of Information Act, I hereby request the following records:

Under the Freedom of Information Act (5 U.S.C. § 552) and the Presidential Records Act (44 U.S.C. §§ 2201–2207), I hereby request access to and copies of records maintained by the Barack Obama Presidential Library relating to the death and state funeral of former Georgian President and Soviet Foreign Minister Eduard Shevardnadze, who died on July 7, 2014.

Request Scope
I am seeking all records created or received between July 1, 2014, and July 31, 2014, pertaining to the U.S. government's response to Shevardnadze's death and funeral. This request includes, but is not limited to:

Drafting History: All drafts, revisions, and internal memoranda related to the preparation of any official White House statement on the death of Eduard Shevardnadze, including communications originating from or passing through the Office of the Press Secretary and the National Security Council (NSC) Executive Secretariat.

Official Correspondence: Final versions of any formal letters of condolence or diplomatic cables (including those transmitted via the State Department) from President Barack Obama or Vice President Joseph Biden addressed to the Shevardnadze family, President Giorgi Margvelashvili, Ambassador Archil Gegeshidze, Foreign Minister Maia Panjikidze or Prime Minister Irakli Garibashvili of Georgia.

Briefing Materials: Any NSC "Point Papers," decision memos, or briefing memoranda prepared for the President or Vice President concerning Eduard Shevardnadze's historical legacy, the significance of his funeral, and the diplomatic implications for U.S.-Georgian and U.S.-Russian relations.

Technical Specifications
To facilitate a thorough search, I request that the library examine the following record systems:

WHORM (White House Office of Records Management) Subject Files under code CO054 (Georgia) and FO (Foreign Affairs) for the specified date range;
NSC Subject Files and Country Files categorized under Georgia or the former Soviet Union/Russia;
Any applicable ME (Messages) subject files relating to condolences transmitted during this period;
The Alpha Files of the WHORM system for correspondence addressed to or received from Georgian government officials.

If digital records are identified, please provide associated metadata, including timestamps and sender/recipient fields.

Suggested Search Terms and Keywords
To assist archival staff in conducting electronic and full-text searches of relevant databases, I suggest the following keywords and phrases be applied across all applicable record systems:

Eduard Shevardnadze (including variant spellings: Shevardnadze, Ševardnadze)
Georgia / Georgian government / Republic of Georgia
Condolence / condolences / letter of condolence / message of sympathy
Giorgi Margvelashvili (President of Georgia)
Archil Gegeshidze (Georgian Ambassador to the United States)
Maia Panjikidze (Georgian Foreign Minister)
Irakli Garibashvili (Prime Minister of Georgia)
Soviet Foreign Minister / Cold War / glasnost / perestroika
Tbilisi / Gori (Shevardnadze's funeral was held in Tbilisi)
U.S.-Georgia relations / U.S.-Georgian relations
NSC / National Security Council / Senior Director for Russia and Eurasia
State Department / cable / diplomatic cable / démarche
July 2014 / funeral / state funeral

I am requesting these records in my capacity as an independent researcher focused on diplomatic history and U.S.-Georgian relations. Disclosure of these records is in the public interest because they document how the United States government managed the legacy of a pivotal Cold War figure during a period of acute geopolitical sensitivity — namely, the aftermath of Russia's annexation of Crimea in March 2014. This information is not primarily in my commercial interest. Accordingly, I request a waiver of all processing fees pursuant to 5 U.S.C. § 552(a)(4)(A)(iii).

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 20 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Robert Delaware
